content.claim: Hungary exhibits the highest level of student segregation by socioeconomic advantage in the EU (2022 PISA), with a 53-point performance gap between children of graduates and non-graduates compared to the EU average of 33 points.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Association between segregation and track system is correlation; causality is not directly demonstrated in cited sources
Performance gap reflects multiple policy and structural factors beyond tracking alone
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
